accuracy_full_suite.basis: 511 function pairs (52 core + 160 expanded + 150 scaled + 149 real-world)
accuracy_full_suite.methodology: Each pair encoded via σ-bridge into QF_BV constraints, solved with Z3

accuracy_core_pairs.basis: 52 core function pairs covering arithmetic, division, shift, cast, loops, error handling

accuracy_combined_212.basis: 212 combined function pairs (core + expanded + memory + c2rust)

k_sensitivity.conclusion: K=32 provides optimal accuracy/performance tradeoff; diminishing returns beyond K=32

theorems: Lemma 1: σ-Bridge Coercion Correctness (case analysis on 7 divergence classes)
Lemma 2: Simulation Preservation (case analysis on alignment kinds)
Theorem 1: Product Program Soundness (UNSAT ⟹ equivalence on well-defined inputs)
Theorem 2: Completeness Characterization (conditions C1-C4 for completeness)
Lemma 3: BMC Bound Monotonicity (increasing K preserves results)
Lemma 4: σ-Bridge Coercion Coverage (exhaustive case analysis)
Theorem 3: SAT Implies Witness of Divergence

ir_erasure.thesis: Source-level verification captures UB-defined divergences that IR-level comparison erases

pipeline_stages.ir_lowering: C AST → IR and Rust AST → IR with overflow annotations
pipeline_stages.alignment: LCS-based block + instruction alignment with similarity scoring
pipeline_stages.product_program: Paired execution with σ-bridge coercion insertion
